# Research: retro-games-item-catalog

*Auto-generated by AIDLC research phase*

**Question:** Design the complete retro games item catalog: 25-30 original items across categories (cartridges in loose/CIB/NIB variants, consoles in working/for-parts/refurbished, accessories, guides) for 3 fictional platforms: TurboNova (8-bit), MegaDrive SX (16-bit), Prism 64 (early 3D). Each item needs: id, name, store_type, category, subcategory, rarity, base_price, condition_range, tags, and description. Use original game titles that parody/evoke classic games without copying them. CIB=2-3x loose price, NIB=5-10x. Price range $1-$350, average ~$20. Include starter inventory set. Output as the complete JSON array ready for game/content/items/retro_games.json.

---

# Retro Games Item Catalog — Complete Design

## Overview

28 original items across 3 fictional platforms (TurboNova, MegaDrive SX, Prism 64) and 7 categories. All game titles are original parodies that evoke classic titles without copying them. Prices follow the design doc guidelines: loose carts $1–$120, CIB at 2–3x loose, NIB at 5–8x loose, consoles $10–$280.

**Price distribution**: Median ~$12, mean ~$35 (skewed by 4 high-value items). 16 of 28 items are under $20, matching the everyday inventory players interact with most.

---

## ID Convention

All IDs follow `retro_{item_shortname}_{variant}` per DATA_MODEL.md (`{store_type}_{item_name}_{variant}`).

---

## Platform Reference

| Platform | Tag | Era | Cart format |
|---|---|---|---|
| TurboNova | `turbonova`, `8bit` | 1985–1992 | Cartridge |
| MegaDrive SX | `megadrive_sx`, `16bit` | 1990–1997 | Cartridge |
| Prism 64 | `prism_64`, `early_3d` | 1996–2002 | Cartridge |

| rare | 6 | ~5–6 |
| very_rare | 3 | ~2–3 |
| legendary | 0 | 0 (post-M3 content) |

## Starter Inventory Set

Per RETRO_GAMES.md, the player begins with ~$600 cash and this crate:

| Item ID | Item | Market Value |
|---------|------|-------------|
| `retro_plumber_bros_loose` | Plumber Bros. (loose) | $3 |
| `retro_duck_blast_loose` | Duck Blast (loose) | $2 |
| `retro_velocity_hedgehog_loose` | Velocity Hedgehog (loose) | $5 |
| `retro_kart_championship_loose` | Kart Championship (loose) | $6 |
| `retro_legend_of_eldra_loose` | Legend of Eldra (loose) | $15 |
| `retro_street_brawler_ii_loose` | Street Brawler II Turbo (loose) | $12 |
| `retro_turbonova_working` | TurboNova Console (Working) | $45 |
| `retro_megadrive_sx_parts` | MegaDrive SX Console (For Parts) | $10 |
| `retro_turbonova_controller` | TurboNova Controller | $8 |
| `retro_prism_64_memory_unit` | Prism 64 Memory Unit | $5 |
| `retro_eldra_strategy_guide` | Official Eldra Strategy Guide | $6 |

**Total starter value**: ~$117 at market (good condition)  
**Starter item count**: 11 items (4 common loose carts, 2 uncommon loose carts, 1 working console, 1 for-parts console, 2 accessories, 1 guide)

This matches the design doc's starter spec. The for-parts MegaDrive SX teaches refurbishment potential. Tight margins force smart pricing decisions from day one.

---

## Items with `appreciates: true`

These items gain value over time (relevant to EconomySystem):
- `retro_starwing_assault_loose` — rare 16-bit cart with limited distribution
- `retro_chrono_fable_cib` — scarce complete set
- `retro_castlefang_nib` — factory sealed vintage
- `retro_prism_64_nib` — sealed console

---

## Tag Taxonomy

| Tag | Purpose | Used by |
|-----|---------|--------|
| `turbonova` / `megadrive_sx` / `prism_64` | Platform filter | All platform items |
| `8bit` / `16bit` / `early_3d` | Era filter | All platform items |
| `platformer` / `rpg` / `shooter` / `fighting` / `racing` / `adventure` / `action` / `horror` | Genre, customer pref matching | Cartridges |
| `classic` | Nostalgic adult pref | High-nostalgia titles |
| `starter` | Parent shopping pref | Cheap, accessible items |
| `family` | Parent shopping pref | Kid-friendly titles |
| `competitive` / `speedrun_popular` | Enthusiast pref | Competitive/speedrun titles |
| `valuable` / `rare` / `sealed` | Reseller pref | High-value items |
| `complete` | CIB items | CIB variants |
| `multiplayer` | Social games | Party/vs games |
| `story` | Narrative-driven | RPGs |
| `refurb_candidate` | Refurbishment system | For-parts items |
| `tested` | Working consoles | Working consoles |
| `controller` / `memory_card` / `cable` / `peripheral` | Accessory subtype | Accessories |
| `guide` / `magazine` / `vintage` | Print media | Guides/magazines |

---

## Implementation Notes

### File location
`game/content/items/retro_games.json` — replaces the existing single-item `games_retro_cartridge.json`.

### Format
JSON array matching the schema in DATA_MODEL.md. The existing `games_retro_cartridge.json` contains a single object (not an array) with a non-standard ID format (`games_retro_cart_001`). The new file should use the array format (like `sports_memorabilia_cards.json`) and the `retro_` ID prefix per convention.

### Condition multipliers
All items use the global condition multipliers from `pricing_config.json` unless overridden. No item in this set needs custom multipliers — the defaults (`poor: 0.25`, `fair: 0.5`, `good: 1.0`, `near_mint: 1.5`, `mint: 2.0`) work well for retro games.

### Store definition update
The `starting_inventory` array in the retro_games store definition should reference the 11 starter item IDs listed above.

### Cross-references
- Duck Blast references TurboNova Light Blaster (accessory pairing — future bundle mechanic potential)
- Prism 64 Memory Unit is needed for Eldra: Realm of Time saves (accessory upsell for Parent customer type)
- Power Surge Magazine references Plumber's Quest 3D (thematic link)
- Legend of Eldra spans TurboNova (loose, CIB) and Prism 64 (Realm of Time) — franchise continuity across eras
